active_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.optimize.dual_annealing for global optimization with smooth constraint reformulation
active_paradigms.description: Apply scipy.optimize.dual_annealing which performs global stochastic optimization and is more robust to non-smooth constraints. Reformulate non-overlap constraints using squared distances: (xi-xj)² + (yi-yj)² >= (ri+rj)² instead of sqrt-based constraints. This eliminates the gradient singularity. Use the same variable packing (3n variables for x, y, r) and bounds. Set maxiter=2000, initial_temp=5230, and restart_temp_ratio=0.0001 for thorough global search. The callback mechanism can track the best solution found during annealing.
active_paradigms.cautions: dual_annealing minimizes, so negate the objective. Use bounds parameter for variable limits. The constraint violation needs to be handled via penalty function since dual_annealing doesn't support explicit constraints - add large penalty for constraint violations in the objective.

active_paradigms.idea: Implement iterative coordinate expansion physics simulation with adaptive time steps
active_paradigms.description: Build a pure physics-based approach without any optimization library. Initialize circles at well-spaced positions with small equal radii. In each iteration: (1) compute repulsion forces between overlapping circles proportional to overlap distance, (2) compute wall repulsion forces for circles touching boundaries, (3) update positions using these forces with adaptive damping, (4) expand all radii by a small factor, (5) resolve any remaining overlaps by shrinking the offending circles. Use 10000+ iterations with gradually decreasing expansion rate. Track the best valid configuration throughout.
active_paradigms.what_to_optimize: sum of radii through iterative expansion, constraint satisfaction through physics
active_paradigms.cautions: Need careful tuning of force constants and damping. Must ensure circles never leave the square. Use adaptive time stepping - smaller steps when near constraint boundaries. Track best solution since physics may oscillate.

active_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.optimize.shgo (simplicial homology global optimization) with penalty-based formulation
active_paradigms.description: Apply scipy.optimize.shgo which uses simplicial homology to find global minima in non-convex problems. It's designed for problems with expensive constraints and can handle non-smooth regions better than gradient methods. Reformulate the problem as unconstrained with penalty terms: objective = -sum(ri) + P*sum(max(0, ri+rj - dist_ij)²) + P*sum(max(0, ri - xi)² + max(0, ri - yi)² + max(0, xi + ri - 1)² + max(0, yi + ri - 1)²). Use sampling methods like 'sobol' for better coverage of the search space with n=200 sampling points.
active_paradigms.what_to_optimize: sum of radii with implicit constraint handling via penalties
active_paradigms.cautions: shgo requires bounds for all variables. The penalty coefficient P needs to be large enough (e.g., 1000) to enforce constraints but not so large it causes numerical issues. Use the 'worst' argument to continue searching even after finding a local minimum.

tried_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.optimize.minimize with SLSQP for joint position-radius optimization
tried_paradigms.description: Treat all 78 variables (x, y, r for each of 26 circles) as continuous optimization variables. Objective: minimize negative sum of radii. Constraints: (1) boundary constraints x_i - r_i >= 0, x_i + r_i <= 1, y_i - r_i >= 0, y_i + r_i <= 1 as inequalities, (2) non-overlap constraints sqrt((x_i-x_j)^2 + (y_i-y_j)^2) - r_i - r_j >= 0 for all pairs. Use SLSQP method which handles nonlinear constraints. Initialize from current hexagonal pattern. Run multiple optimizations with perturbed starting points (add small random noise to initial positions) to escape local minima. Use 1000-2000 max iterations.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Primary metric: sum of radii (target ratio to 2.635)
tried_paradigms.cautions: Non-overlap constraints are non-convex, so multiple restarts are essential. Use constraint tolerance of 1e-6 to match evaluator. Ensure radii stay positive with bounds. The problem has many local optima, so try at least 10 different initializations.

tried_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.spatial.Voronoi for geometric radius maximization
tried_paradigms.description: Exploit Voronoi diagram structure to determine optimal circle positions. Initialize points using current pattern. Compute Voronoi diagram with scipy.spatial.Voronoi. For each point, the maximum inscribed circle radius equals the minimum distance to: (1) Voronoi cell edges (determined by neighboring points), and (2) square boundaries. This gives exact upper bounds on radii. Then optimize point positions to maximize sum of these radii: for each point, move it toward the centroid of its Voronoi cell to increase minimum distance to edges. Iterate Voronoi computation and point movement until convergence. Use scipy.optimize.minimize on point positions only (52 variables) with radii computed analytically from Voronoi structure.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Sum of radii computed from Voronoi cell geometry
tried_paradigms.cautions: Handle boundary points carefully - their Voronoi cells extend outside the square. For points near edges, clip Voronoi cells to the unit square. Use scipy.spatial.distance.cdist for efficient distance computations. Ridge points and ridge vertices from Voronoi need careful indexing.

tried_paradigms.idea: Physics-based iterative expansion without optimization library
tried_paradigms.description: Use a simulation approach where circles expand and push each other apart. Start with small circles (r=0.01) at good initial positions. Iteratively: (1) Expand all radii by small factor (1.01x), (2) For each overlapping pair, move circles apart along the line connecting their centers by half the overlap distance, (3) For circles outside boundaries, project them back inside and reduce their radius to fit, (4) Repeat until no more expansion possible. This mimics physical expansion of gas bubbles and finds locally optimal packings without formal optimization. Use numpy for efficient vectorized distance computations. Run multiple simulations with different initial arrangements.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Final sum of radii after simulation converges
tried_paradigms.cautions: Use small expansion factors (1.001-1.01) for stability. Limit iterations to prevent infinite loops. Handle degenerate cases where circles have zero valid radius. Use numpy vectorization for O(n^2) distance computations efficiently. Multiple random initializations needed since this is a local method.

tried_paradigms.idea: Geometric construction with corner-first placement strategy
tried_paradigms.description: Build the packing explicitly using geometric principles. Start by placing 4 large circles at corners with radius ≈ 0.207 (touching two walls). Then place 8 circles along edges (one between each corner pair). Fill the remaining 14 interior circles using hexagonal packing pattern. Calculate radii from contact constraints: corner circles touch two walls and potentially one neighbor, edge circles touch one wall and neighbors, interior circles touch neighbors only. This exploits the geometric insight that circles with fewer wall constraints can be larger.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: sum_radii by maximizing individual circle sizes through strategic placement
tried_paradigms.cautions: Verify all constraints are satisfied; use geometric calculations for exact contact points; the pattern must fit 26 circles total (4 corners + 8 edges + 14 interior)

tried_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.optimize.minimize with trust-constr method for better constraint handling
tried_paradigms.description: Replace SLSQP with trust-constr which handles nonlinear constraints more robustly. Trust-constr uses a trust-region approach that can better navigate the complex constraint landscape of circle packing. Implement analytical Jacobians for constraints (both boundary and non-overlap) to improve convergence. Use sparse constraint Jacobian since each constraint only involves 2 circles. The method approximates the Hessian of the Lagrangian, which can help escape poor local minima that trap SLSQP.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: sum_radii through improved constrained optimization convergence
tried_paradigms.cautions: Trust-constr requires constraints in a different format; implement constraint Jacobians for efficiency; may need more iterations than SLSQP; use 'lanczos' or 'svd' for subspace method

tried_paradigms.description: Apply differential_evolution as a global optimizer that doesn't rely on gradients and can escape local minima. This is well-suited for non-convex problems with many constraints. Use the 'best1bin' or 'randtobest1bin' strategy. Wrap constraints using the penalty method or the new constraint interface in scipy. The population-based approach explores the solution space more broadly than gradient-based methods, potentially finding better packings that SLSQP misses.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: sum_radii through global search avoiding local minima traps
tried_paradigms.cautions: Differential evolution is slower per iteration; use appropriate population size (15-20 per dimension); set maxiter high enough (1000+); constraints must be handled via penalty or wrapper; bounds are naturally supported

tried_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.optimize.minimize with trust-constr method and squared distance constraints
tried_paradigms.description: Replace SLSQP with trust-constr method which is specifically designed for constrained optimization with better convergence properties. Reformulate non-overlap constraints using squared distances: (x_i - x_j)^2 + (y_i - y_j)^2 >= (r_i + r_j)^2 instead of sqrt-based constraints. This avoids numerical instability near zero and provides more robust gradients. Trust-constr uses a trust-region approach that handles non-linear constraints better than SLSQP, potentially finding better local optima. Use the same hexagonal initial positions but increase restarts to 20.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: sum of radii by finding better local optima through improved constraint handling
tried_paradigms.cautions: trust-constr requires constraint Jacobians for efficiency - provide analytical gradients. Squared constraints must maintain consistent inequality direction. Use 'verbose': 0 to avoid excessive output.

tried_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.optimize.differential_evolution for global optimization with penalty method
tried_paradigms.description: Apply differential_evolution which is a population-based global optimizer that doesn't rely on gradients and can escape local optima. Use bounds for all 78 variables (positions in [0.01, 0.99], radii in [0.01, 0.5]). Implement a penalty function that adds large penalties for constraint violations: penalty = sum of max(0, r_i + r_j - dist_ij)^2 for overlaps plus boundary violations. The objective becomes -sum(radii) + penalty_weight * penalty. Use strategy='best1bin', popsize=15, maxiter=500, mutation=(0.5, 1.0), recombination=0.7 for good exploration-exploitation balance.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: sum of radii by globally exploring the solution space rather than converging to local optima
tried_paradigms.cautions: Penalty weight must be large enough (e.g., 10000) to ensure constraints are satisfied in the final solution. Validate the solution afterward and use polish=True for local refinement.

tried_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.optimize.basinhopping with constrained local minimization
tried_paradigms.description: Apply basinhopping which combines local minimization with random jumps to escape local minima. Use SLSQP or trust-constr as the local minimizer with all constraints. Configure niter=100, T=0.5 for temperature, stepsize=0.1 for random perturbations. Implement a custom take_step function that perturbs positions by small amounts while respecting bounds. The algorithm will explore multiple basins of attraction, potentially finding the global optimum. Use the best hexagonal-based initial position with small random perturbation.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: sum of radii by stochastic exploration of multiple local minima basins
tried_paradigms.cautions: Basinhopping can be slow - use appropriate niter and check timeout. The accept_test callback should reject solutions that violate constraints. Save the best solution found across all iterations.
